prompt:
We demo our embedded display library with little games, and I need a snake engine that's completely deterministic so the demo reel renders the same every run. No timers, no randomness — the game advances only when the caller ticks it, and food comes from a queue baked into a fixture file. Build snake.py, stdlib only.

Fixture format (blank lines ignored): line 1 'WIDTH HEIGHT'; line 2 the snake as x y pairs, head first; line 3 the starting direction N/E/S/W; every following line is 'X Y' — the food queue, in the order it will appear. The first queued food is on the board from the start; each time it's eaten the next one appears immediately, and when the queue runs dry there's simply no food on the board. `load(path)` parses that and returns a Game; `Game(width, height, segments, direction, food_queue)` builds one directly. Validate on construction: boards smaller than 2 in either dimension, an empty or self-overlapping snake, segments off the board, or a bad direction are ValueErrors (load also rejects a snake line with an odd number of coordinates or a fixture missing its three header lines).

Game state: `segments` (list of (x, y), head first), `direction`, `food` ((x, y) or None), `score`, `alive`. `tick(command=None)` advances one step: the command (N/E/S/W) changes heading first, except that turning straight back into your own neck is ignored when the snake is longer than one segment (a lone head may reverse). Junk commands are ValueErrors. Then the head moves one cell: onto the food = grow (tail stays put, score +1, next food spawns); otherwise the tail vacates its cell — which is exactly why stepping into the spot your tail is leaving THIS tick is legal. Hitting a wall or any other body cell kills the snake: alive goes False, the board stays exactly as it was before the fatal move, and any further tick() raises RuntimeError.

`render()` returns the board framed in '+'/'-'/'|': '@' head, '#' body, '*' food, '.' empty — the tests diff whole frame sequences against move scripts, character for character.

Acceptance contract: test_snake.py with the three snake*.txt fixtures. python3 test_snake.py must pass; don't touch the tests or fixtures.
